title: Server --help and zero-config workflow loading
purpose: Running aion-server --help outputs a JSON error log instead of clean help text, making it impossible to discover available flags. Additionally, the --workflow-package flag crashes when used without --config because the visibility store is not configured in zero-config mode. These two issues block the getting-started experience: a developer cannot discover how to run the server or load a workflow without a full config file.
task: Fix the server binary so --help produces clean clap-formatted help text instead of JSON error output. Ensure --workflow-package works without --config by falling back to in-memory defaults for the visibility store and any other required configuration. Document the --workflow-package flag in dev-config.toml as a comment showing the zero-config usage pattern.

requirements.title: Proper --help output via clap
requirements.spec: WHEN a user runs aion-server --help, THE SYSTEM SHALL display clean, clap-formatted help text listing all available flags and subcommands. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT emit JSON error logs or tracing output as part of the help text.
acceptance: aion-server --help prints structured help text with flag descriptions.
No JSON log lines appear in the help output.
No tracing initialization occurs before help is displayed.
Exit code is 0 after printing help.

requirements.title: Workflow-package works without --config
requirements.spec: WHEN a user runs aion-server --workflow-package <path> without providing --config, THE SYSTEM SHALL start successfully using in-memory defaults for the event store, visibility store, and any other required configuration rather than crashing.
acceptance: The server loads the workflow package and registers the workflow type.
An in-memory event store and visibility store are used when no config file is provided.
The server is usable for local development without creating a config file.

requirements.title: Document --workflow-package in dev-config.toml
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL add a comment block to dev-config.toml showing the zero-config usage pattern with --workflow-package, so developers can discover it without reading source code.
acceptance: dev-config.toml contains a comment block explaining --workflow-package usage.
The comment shows the minimal command to start the server with a workflow package.
The comment explains that --config is optional when using --workflow-package.

boundaries: SHALL NOT change the config file format or break existing config files.
SHALL NOT remove the --config flag or change its behavior when provided.
SHALL NOT change the default production behavior — zero-config is a dev convenience only.
SHALL NOT add new CLI frameworks — continue using clap.
verification: Manual test: aion-server --help prints clean help text with no JSON logs.
Manual test: aion-server --workflow-package hello-world.aion starts without --config.
